title: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Coordinator
California Department of Technology
P.O. Box 1810
Rancho Cordova, CA 95741

Dear Public Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to California's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including the Poppy GenAI platform developed by the California Department of Technology.

We are requesting the following records under Cal. Gov. Code § 6253(c):

1. All interaction logs from Poppy, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from September 25, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Poppy or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for Poppy showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Poppy is an enterprise generative AI platform built by CDT for California state employees, utilizing 11 different AI models and grounded on public state data. As of early 2026, Poppy has more than 2,600 users across 66 departments, with over 20 state departments involved in its development and testing (see California Department of Technology, "Poppy: California's Digital Assistant," https://www.genai.ca.gov/poppy/; see also StateScoop, "Only California state employees can use Poppy, a new AI assistant designed for security," https://statescoop.com/california-poppy-state-government-ai-assistant/; GovTech, "California Looks to Drive Efficiency With AI Assistant," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/california-looks-to-drive-efficiency-with-ai-assistant). Governor Newsom has also launched initiatives to accelerate responsible AI in state government (see Governor of California, Dec. 16, 2025, https://www.gov.ca.gov/2025/12/16/governor-newsom-launches-new-initiatives-to-partner-with-tech-policy-experts-and-accelerate-responsible-ai-in-state-government/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The California Public Records Act defines "public records" to include any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business, regardless of physical form or characteristics, encompassing electronic communications and digital files. Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530. Governor Newsom's Executive Order N-12-23 (September 2023) directed state agencies to develop a generative AI framework, and the CalGovOps GenAI Policy Framework (2024) requires agencies to maintain inventories of AI/GenAI tools and document use cases. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: AI Policies and Emails

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: AI Policies and Emails
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any policies relating to the usage of Large language models, and other "AI".

Retention policies of AI/LLM chat logs and image creations.

From January 1 2022 to the present:

1) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Spokane Public Schools employees and any contracted workers and the following domains:

@openai.com
@chatgpt.com
@anthropic.com
@claude.ai
@gab.ai
@jasper.ai

2) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Spokane Public Schools employees and any contracted workers and the following addresses:
m365copilotupdates@microsoft.com
microsoft365copilot@communication.microsoft.com
google-gemini-noreply@google.com

To make things simpler, this request does not include the contents of the emails. The information I am hoping for is: Recipient, sender, date received/sent, and subject line.

I believe this should be exportable via Google Vault or Microsoft Purview.

If your agency has a retention policy for this data shorter than this I would be grateful if you informed me so I can adjust my request time frame.

I would prefer the data in text searchable formats, such as CSV or Excel, if possible.

If you have any questions or wish to clarify any part of this request feel free to let me know. If this request is too wide, feel free to contact me to explore the possibility of adjusting the scope of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

communications.communication:

 Spokane Public Schools (District 81) has received your request for records.

  Request Number: PRR-2026-108

We will respond to your initial request within 5 business days.

However, some requests may require additional time. In accordance with FERPA, requests for student records will be completed within 45 calendar days.

communications.subject: Spokane Public Schools - Public Records Request PRR-2026-108: Records Ready
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor, 
  
 The District has received your request dated 04/22/2026 for "To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: Any policies relating to the usage of Large language models, and other "AI". Retention policies of AI/LLM chat logs and image creations. From January 1 2022 to the present: 1) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Spokane Public Schools employees and any contracted workers and the following domains: @openai.com @chatgpt.com @anthropic.com @claude.ai @gab.ai @jasper.ai 2) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Spokane Public Schools employees and any contracted workers and the following addresses: m365copilotupdates@microsoft.com microsoft365copilot@communication.microsoft.com google-gemini-noreply@google.com To make things simpler, this request does not include the contents of the emails. The information I am hoping for is: Recipient, sender, date received/sent, and subject line. I believe this should be exportable via Google Vault or Microsoft Purview. If your agency has a retention policy for this data shorter than this I would be grateful if you informed me so I can adjust my request time frame. I would prefer the data in text searchable formats, such as CSV or Excel, if possible. If you have any questions or wish to clarify any part of this request feel free to let me know. If this request is too wide, feel free to contact me to explore the possibility of adjusting the scope of this request. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not." 
  
 
The District's search feature for responsive emails does not have a way to export logs of responsive emails as requested. Microsoft holds email logs for 90 days. You state you are not seeking emails, as such we will not search for or provide emails responsive to your request, unless you tell us in 30 days that you would like the District to do an email search for responsive emails. 
  
 The District gathered the requested 90 days for the previous 90 days from files maintained by Technology Services. You have access via the portal to view and download the requested records, please use this link: Spokane Public Schools Records Requests - Track request (justfoia.com). 
  
 Request Number PRR-2026-108 
  
 Security Key 1FDEAC01 
  
 As a security precaution, after 30 days, the access to the records will be removed. To view the records beyond 30 days, please save a copy securely. 
  
 You will see some records have been redacted or blacked out with a text box that has a numbered code (01 through 31) in white writing over the top. The explanation of these codes will be added to the last page of each record that has a redaction. You will know which record or email has been redacted as the word “Redacted” has been added to the end of the title of the record. 
  
 Under Public Records law, a requestor must claim or review assembled records within thirty days of the agency’s notification to him or her that the records are available for inspection or copying. If the requestor fails to claim or review the records within the thirty-day period or make other arrangements, the agency may close the request and re-file the assembled records. 
  
 The District has provided logs of responsive emails within the previous 90 days. Unless we hear otherwise from you, this concludes the District’s response to this request as the District considers this request to be fulfilled and closed. The Public Records Act’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review of the District’s response and records produced has started to run because the District does not intend to further address this request. The one year statute of limitations means you have one year to challenge the District’s response and responsive records released to you. 
  
 If you have follow up questions about this request, please ask within the next 30 days. Please let us know if you believe additional responsive records have not been provided or if we have misunderstood or misinterpreted your request. If you are no longer seeking responsive records to this request please also let us know. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Spokane Public Schools
200 N. Bernard
Spokane, Washington 99201
509-354-7332 
 publicrecordsrequest@spokaneschools.org

title: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Employment information for all sworn law enforcement officers employed from the dates: 1950–present. Specifically, I request the following public information for each officer: Full name, Date of original employment/appointment, position title and history of position changes, dates of promotions, demotions, transfers. 

If possible, please provide this data in electronic format, Excel or CSV, database, via email attachment or CD-ROM if email is not feasible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alex Noll

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Noll,

The County has received your request for employment information concerning sworn law enforcement officers employed from 1950 to present.

Under North Carolina law, certain personnel information is public, including employee name, date of original employment or appointment, current position/title, and the date and type of each promotion, demotion, transfer, suspension, separation, or other change in position classification.

The County will produce responsive public employment information that is currently maintained in its electronic systems.

The County does not maintain a single searchable electronic database of sworn law enforcement employment records dating back to 1950. Records prior to implementation of the County's current electronic HR system are not maintained in a consolidated, exportable format and would require manual file review and reconstruction. The North Carolina Public Records Law does not require a public agency to create new records or reconstruct historical data not maintained in its files.

Based on our current systems, the County is able to provide public employment information maintained electronically. Please let us know whether production of those existing electronic records will satisfy your request, or whether you wish to narrow or clarify the timeframe or scope.

If any costs are associated with production, we will notify you in advance.

With kind regards,
J Kwame Opata
Assistant County Attorney

[cid:image001.png@01DCAA63.C3AE8170]

Sincerely,

Alex Noll

communications.subject: RE: Request for Public Records - Andre Natta
communications.communication:
Good morning,
 Per Section 2-4 of the Tuscaloosa Municipal Code, the City is required to charge for staff time (beyond the first hour of staff time) spent researching and responding to a public records request. City staff has estimated that it will take about 12 hours of staff time to respond to your request. This includes time to research and gather potentially responsive records, to convert those files into a usable format (for example, converting .msg files to .pdf files), and reviewing the records to ensure they are responsive. We estimate the total charges for this staff time to be about $588.84. Please note: this is only an estimate and the total charges may be more or less depending on the length of time it takes to fulfill your requests. This also does not include the additional $10.00 if the responsive documents must be provided on a flash drive or CD/DVD.  Please let me know if you are willing to pay those charges, and we will begin working on the response ASAP.
Public Records
Office of the City Clerk
City of Tuscaloosa
205-248-5010
publicrecords@tuscaloosa.com<mailto:publicrecords@tuscaloosa.com>
